title: HTTP node returns 400 Bad Request with custom auth
symptom: When using custom auth credentials in the HTTP node, the request fails with a 400 Bad Request error.
applies_when: Using custom auth in HTTP node
root_cause: The HTTP request fails because the required 'content-type' header is missing. Without this header, the server cannot correctly interpret the body format, leading to a 400 error.
fix_steps: Add the 'content-type' header with the value 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' to the HTTP node.
Alternatively, include the header in the custom auth configuration.
agent_instruction: Ensure the 'content-type' header is set to 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' in the HTTP node.
